steps: List the documents your station actually has that matter for institutional knowledge.
Before buying anything, ask whether a CLAUDE.md and a few plain folders (level 1) already answer most of your team's questions.
If that's not enough, try dropping the documents into a raw folder and asking Claude to build a wiki (level 2) — no vendor, no database, no subscription.
Only consider semantic search, a knowledge graph, or an always-on platform if you can name the specific pain level 1 or 2 didn't solve.
Whatever you build, keep it as plain files you own, not a vendor's proprietary format.
Name one person as the owner of the brain and set a date for when it gets reviewed and refreshed.
Bring the vendor's pitch back only if your own level-1 or level-2 attempt genuinely fails at something specific.
verify: Ask three staff members to find last year's pledge drive script or the underwriting compliance rule using only what you built — if any of them can't find it in under a minute, the level you're at isn't solving the pain yet.
